hardresissue.description: Specifies when creating a reservation if the reservation can be only a hard reservation.
hardresissue.title: Requires hard reservation on use

storelocsiteid.description: Identifies the site where the storeroom is located.
storelocsiteid.title: Storeroom Site

abctype.description: ABC analysis in Inventory lets you quickly identify which inventory items represent your company's greatest investment in terms of monetary value and turnover rate. The ABC Type value for a item is determined by running an ABC Analysis report, which multiplies the current YTD issued quantity by the last cost of the item. The items are then sorted in descending order of the dollar value reached by this calculation.
abctype.title: ABC Type

status.description: The current status of the item such as ACTIVE, PENDING, PLANNING, PENDOBS and OBSELETE.
status.title: Status

reorder.description: Specify whether to include the item in the inventory reorder process.
reorder.title: Reorder

sstock.description: The quantity of inventory stock items that are kept in storage for unforeseen requirements.
sstock.title: Safety Stock

issueunit.description: Standard quantity by which the item is issued from the storeroom, such as each or roll. Click the Select Value button to view a list of valid issue units.
issueunit.title: Issue Unit

manufacturer.description: Manufacturer code indicating the company that makes this item.
manufacturer.title: Manufacturer

nextinvoicedate.description: The next date that an invoice will be generated if one is needed. When the Frequency and Frequency Units fields are specified, the Next Invoice Date will default based on the current date/time plus the frequency.
nextinvoicedate.title: Next Invoice Date

vendor.description: Vendor code indicating the primary vendor for this item.
vendor.title: Primary Vendor

consignment.description: Select this check box to identify the item as a consignment type of item.
consignment.title: Consignment

minlevel.description: The minimum quantity of inventory stock items required. When the stock balance reaches this number, the inventory items must be reordered.
minlevel.title: Reorder Point

invmtol.description: Percentage by which a physical count can be below the adjusted balance and still be reconciled. Maximo uses tolerance settings to determine the accuracy of the physical count. An item's tolerance values are storeroom-specific. Maximo uses the tolerance value established in the Organizations application as the default value, unless you enter a value in the Minus Count Tolerance field on the Inventory tab.
invmtol.title: Minus Count Tolerance

consvendor.description: The vendor that is responsible for replenishing the consignment items. Purchase orders and invoices are generated for the vendor that is specified in this field.
consvendor.title: Consignment Vendor

invgentype.description: You can specify how the invoice is generated for the current item. You can specify that invoices are generated based on consumption or on frequency or that they are generated manually.
invgentype.title: Invoice Generation Type

orderunit.description: Unit Of Order. A unit can be each, box, roll, linear feet, cubic feet, square feet etc. For example, 1 box is 1 unit, but the box may be a part of a larger inventory issue element, perhaps a case or crate.
orderunit.title: Order Unit

statusdate.description: Date the work order status was last changed.
statusdate.title: Status Date

deliverytime.description: The number of days it takes from ordering an item to receiving the item.
deliverytime.title: Lead Time (Days)

catalogcode.description: Primary vendor's catalog code for this item.
catalogcode.title: Catalog #

frequnit.description: The unit of measure for the frequency value.
frequnit.title: Frequency Units

location.description: The primary location for the selected item. If an item is stored in more than one storeroom location you can select View Availability from the Select Action menu to view balances for all storeroom locations.
location.title: Storeroom

category.description: Specifies whether the item is a stocked (STK), non-stocked (NS), or a special order (SP) item.
category.title: Stock Category

requestedby.description: Name of person requesting the item.
requestedby.title: Requested By

stagedqty.description: The quantity of items that have been staged.
stagedqty.title: Staged Quantity

conditioncode.description: Condition code associated with the reserved item. A condition code allows you to track the balance and value of each item. You can apply different rates to an item as its condition changes from use, such as from new to used. Click the Select Value button to choose a condition code.
conditioncode.title: Condition Code

requesteddate.description: Date the request for this item was made. Click the Select Date and Time button to use the calendar control.
requesteddate.title: Requested Date

actualqty.description: Number of this item already issued against the reservation.
actualqty.title: Actual Quantity

wonum.description: Work order number for which this item is reserved.
wonum.title: Work Order

glaccount.description: The GL Account to be debited on issue.
glaccount.title: GL Account

mrnum.description: Requisition number associated with this transaction. Click the Select Value button to choose a requisition.
mrnum.title: Requisition

tostoreloc.description: The storeroom location the inventory items are charged to.
tostoreloc.title: To Storeroom

wogroup.description: The identifier of the work order that is related to this record.
wogroup.title: Work Order

ponum.description: The purchase order to which the item reservation is associated.
ponum.title: Purchase Order

location.description: Storeroom location of the reserved item.
location.title: Storeroom

ACTION_SAVETHISQUERY.description: Request Schema for save this query

expectedlifedate.description: The date that the location reaches the expected useful life date. This date is calculated by using the location's original installation date and its expected useful life, which is defined by manufacturer or industry standard.
expectedlifedate.title: Expected EOL

reordfromstore.description: The storeroom that all the items in the current storeroom are reordered from.
reordfromstore.title: Reorder Storeroom

shiptolaborcode.description: Default labor code to ship items to when reorders are processed for this storeroom. Click the Select Value button to choose a labor code.
shiptolaborcode.title: Ship to Labor

storeroomparent.description: The parent storeroom of the current storeroom.
storeroomparent.title: Parent Storeroom

pluscduedate.description: Displays the date that the next calibration must be completed by.
pluscduedate.title: Next Calibration Due Date

bimusage.description: Describes how a location is used and controls COBie export behavior
bimusage.title: BIM Location type

siteid.description: Specifies the site where this location or storeroom resides.
siteid.title: Site

isdefault.description: Specifies whether this storeroom is the default storeroom for the site. If the check box is selected, Maximo reserves items for this storeroom if a job plan or work order does not specify a storeroom. If the check box is cleared (the default), this storeroom is not used as the default storeroom when work orders are generated with no storeroom information.
isdefault.title: Default Storeroom

receiptvaracc.description: Debit account to track the variance between PO and Invoice exchange rates. This account contains the differences between the inventory standard cost and the receipt cost (specific to the storeroom) for any items in the associated storeroom location. It is used for standard costing only. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
receiptvaracc.title: Receipt Variance Account

reordfromstoresiteid.description: The site of the storeroom that all the items in the current storeroom are reordered from.
reordfromstoresiteid.title: Reorder Site

useinpopr.description: Specifies whether this storeroom can be used to supply and reserve inventory items and tools for use with internal purchase requisitions and internal purchase orders. If the check box is selected, you can order and reserve items or tools from this storeroom on internal POs and PRs. If the check box is cleared (the default), this storeroom cannot be used to supply items or tools internally.
useinpopr.title: Use in PO/PR

modelid.description: Import ID Typically from a BIM model or COBie data set
modelid.title: Import ID

pluscloop.description: Select this check box to identify the location as a loop calibration.
pluscloop.title: Loop Calibration

shiptoaddresscode.description: Default address code to ship items to when reorders are processed for this storeroom. Click the Select Value button to choose a shipping code.
shiptoaddresscode.title: Ship to Address

oldshrinkageacc.description: Last shrinkage cost account for manual adjustment for inventory quantity.
oldshrinkageacc.title: Old Shrinkage Account

estendoflife.description: The date when the location reaches its end of life. This date is based on the condition of the location.
estendoflife.title: Estimated EOL

purchvaracc.description: Account used to track fluctuations in purchase costs for items (or the variance between PO and original Last and Standard price). Not used with any MAXIMO transactions. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
purchvaracc.title: Purchase Variance Account

saddresscode.description: The address code identifies a service address. It must be unique by site for each service address.
saddresscode.title: Service Address

shrinkageacc.description: Credit account used when manually adjusting the inventory quantity. The transaction is positive when the actual inventory quantity is greater than the MAXIMO-calculated current balance. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
shrinkageacc.title: Shrinkage Account

oldinvcostadjacc.description: Last inventory cost adjustment account for manual inventory price adjustments.
oldinvcostadjacc.title: Old Invoice Cost Adjustment Account

description.description: Describes the storeroom location. To enter or view additional information, click the Long Description button.
description.title: Description

toolcontrolacc.description: Default debit account for capitalized tools. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
toolcontrolacc.title: Tool Control Account

pluscpmextdate.description: When this check box is selected, the Extended Date on the PM for the location was set. This check box is cleared when the work order is generated.
pluscpmextdate.title: Due Date Extended

invoicevaracc.description: Debit account used to track variances in the price, expressed in the vendor's currency, between receipt and invoice for this storeroom. The transaction amount is positive when the invoice line cost is greater than the receipt cost. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
invoicevaracc.title: Invoice Variance Account

description_longdescription.description: Long Description for Asset Short Description (One Line)
description_longdescription.title: Details

bimroomname.description: The name or number of the room or space
bimroomname.title: Room Name/Number

expectedlife.description: Expected life of the location, expressed in years.  This value is based on the typical useful life that a manufacturer or industry standard recommends for a location or location type.
expectedlife.title: Expected Life

isrepairfacility.description: Specifies whether the location is a repair facility location. Repair facilities can take ownership of work orders from multiple sites in the same organization. User security can then be configured to give permissions to view work orders in multiple sites if the work orders are owned by the repair facility.
isrepairfacility.title: Is a Repair Facility

statusdate.description: Date the Locations status was last changed.
statusdate.title: Status Date

curvaracc.description: Account containing the differences between the PO price and the invoice price that result from changes in the exchange rate. If, as a result of an exchange rate change, the invoice cost in base currency exceeds the receipt cost in base currency, then the transaction amount is positive. If the invoice cost in base currency is less than the receipt cost in base currency, then the transaction amount is negative. This account is paired with the GL control account; when the currency variance account's value decreases, the inventory control account's value decreases. Variance accounts track price variances by storeroom location, not by item. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
curvaracc.title: Currency Variance Account

controlacc.description: Default GL account that Maximo applies to items in this storeroom during GL transactions such as issues, returns, receipts, transfers, and adjustments. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
controlacc.title: GL Control Account

invcostadjacc.description: Account that tracks changes in either the average price or the standard price from manual inventory price adjustments. These changes result from using the Adjust Average Cost and Adjust Standard Cost actions, respectively. Click the Select Value button to choose a GL account.
invcostadjacc.title: Cost Adjustment Account

location.description: Storeroom where the item is stored.
location.title: Location

autowogen.description: Flag that indicates whether to start the wogen process when meter frequency is reached for a location
autowogen.title: Automatically Generate Work Orders

bimimportsrc.description: The external source for the data in this record such as a COBie sheet or an IFC class name
bimimportsrc.title: Import source

pluscismte.description: Defines whether a Tool is a standard used for measuring and testing equipment.
pluscismte.title: Is M&TE

description.description: Describes the condition code associated with this item. A condition code allows you to track the balance and value of each item.
description.title: Description

condrate.description: Condition's rate expressed as a percentage. For example, a condition code of NEW would have 100 as the condition rate (in percent), while a condition code of FAIR might have 50 as the condition rate.
condrate.title: Condition Rate

stocktype.description: Stock type of this condition code
stocktype.title: Stock Type

hasld.description: Boolean flag to indicate if there is any long description for this record
hasld.title: Has Long Description

conditioncode.description: Condition code associated with the item. A condition code allows you to track the balance and value of each item. You can apply different rates to an item as its condition changes from use, such as from new to used. If you mark an item as condition enabled, you must assign at least one condition code to it. For each condition-enabled item, one condition code must have a ""full-value"" rate of 100%. Maximo uses this condition code as the reference point to calculate values for any other condition codes for this item. Each time the item is issued, transferred, or returned you specify the condition of the item based on codes you set up. Click the Detail Menu button to choose a condition code or go the Condition Codes application to create one.
conditioncode.title: Condition Code

pluscisinhousecal.description: When selected, indicates that this tool is calibrated in house. This field can be used to sort tools that are calibrated internally or externally.
pluscisinhousecal.title: Internal Calibration

iskit.description: Specifies whether the item is a kit item that contains a collection of items that can be issued together as a single item. If the check box is selected, the item is a kit. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item is not a kit.
iskit.title: Kit

receipttolerance.description: The percentage value of items that can be received over the amount set in the initial agreement.
receipttolerance.title: Receipt Tolerance %

rolldown.description: Checkbox to indicate if the status change needs to be rolled down to Organization/Inventory.
rolldown.title: RollDown

maxissue.description: Total maximum quantity that can be issued to an asset.
maxissue.title: Maximum Quantity Issued

groupname.description: Identifies the meter group, or category of meters, to which the meter applied to this item belongs. Applies only to rotating items. Click the Select Value button to choose a meter group.
groupname.title: Meter Group

in4.description: Is Item a Standing Order Item?
in4.title: Extra Field 4

taxexempt.description: Select this check box if the item, the tool, or the service is not taxable. The buyer for your organization determines the tax-exempt status of items, of tools, and of services.
taxexempt.title: Tax Exempt

lottype.description: Specifies whether the item is tracked by lot (LOT) or not (NOLOT). LOT is used for an item that is lotted, typically has an expiration date, and is able to be assigned lot numbers upon receipt. NOLOT means that the item does not need to be tracked by lots. Click the Select Value button to choose a lot type.
lottype.title: Lot Type

itemnum.description: Identifies the item record. This value must be unique for all item records. Click the Detail Menu button to view items by classification or availability.
itemnum.title: Item

inspectionrequired.description: Specifies whether the item should be held out of inventory when being received, until the purchase order receipt has been approved. If the check box is selected, the item cannot be received until it has been inspected. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item can be received without an inspection.
inspectionrequired.title: Inspect on Receipt

pluscismteclass.description: When the Is M&TE? field is checked then you have the option to choose a classification for M&TE.
pluscismteclass.title: M&TE Classification

metername.description: Identifies the meter associated with the item, for which a reading is to be recorded when this item is issued to an asset. A meter is a device designed to measure time, distance, speed, or intensity, or indicate and record or regulate the amount or volume, as of the flow of a gas or an electric current. Only non-rotating items can be associated with a meter.
metername.title: Meter

outside.description: Specifies whether the item is a consignment good. If the check box is selected, the item belongs to a contractor (or vendor), but it is stored on-site, and it is an item for which you expect to be charged for its use. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item is not owned by an outside party.
outside.title: Outside

itemsetid.description: Identifies the item set, or group of items, to which this item belongs. An organization is assigned to only one item set, but multiple organizations can use the same item set. Click the Select Value button to choose an item set.
itemsetid.title: Item Set

iscrew.description: Specifies whether the tool can be used in Crew applications and Work Order Tracking applications. If the check box is selected, the tool can be used. If the check box is cleared, the tool cannot be used.
iscrew.title: Crew

hardresissue.description: Indicates whether a hard reservation must be referenced when the item is issued from a storeroom.
hardresissue.title: Requires hard reservation on use

status.description: The current status of the item such as ACTIVE, PENDING, PLANNING, PENDOBS and OBSELETE.
status.title: Status

commodity.description: Identifies the commodity code associated with this item or tool. Click the Select Value button to view a list of valid commodity codes.
commodity.title: Commodity Code

rotating.description: Specifies whether the item is a rotating asset. If the check box is selected, the item becomes an asset tracked by item number and individual asset number. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item is not tracked as an individual asset.
rotating.title: Rotating

commoditygroup.description: Identifies the commodity group, or product category, associated with this item or tool. Click the Select Value button to view a list of valid commdity groups.
commoditygroup.title: Commodity Group

description.description: Names or describes the inventory item. For additional information, click the Long Description button.
description.title: Description

msdsnum.description: Material Safety Data Sheet number issued by the Occupational Safety and Health Association (OSHA). The Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS), which describes product dangers, must be available to purchasers and users of commercial quantities of all hazardous materials.
msdsnum.title: MSDS

issueunit.description: Unit of measure used to issue the item from the storeroom, such as EACH or ROLL. Click the Select Value button to choose an issue unit.
issueunit.title: Issue Unit

sparepartautoadd.description: Specifies whether the item will be added as a spare part for the asset when issued, if the item is not already a spare part. If the checkbox is selected, the item can be added as a spare part. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item is not used as a spare part.
sparepartautoadd.title: Add as Spare Part

attachonissue.description: Specifies whether to attach the item to the parent asset when the item is issued. If the check box is selected, the item becomes a subassembly of the parent asset upon issue. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item is not attached to the parent asset when issued.
attachonissue.title: Attach to Parent Asset on Issue

conditionenabled.description: Specifies whether this item has a value or worth you want to assign and track based on its physical condition. You can apply different rates to an item as its condition changes from use, such as from new to used. If the check box is selected, you must assign at least one full value (100%) condition code for this item, and any other codes you assign must be a percentage of that. If the check box is cleared (the default), the physical condition of the item is not tracked.
conditionenabled.title: Condition Enabled

description.description: Description of a specified vendor catalogue part
description.title: Description

lastdate.description: Last date the item, tool, or service was ordered from this vendor. Click the Select Date button to use the calendar control.
lastdate.title: Last Order Date

manufacturer.description: Manufacturer of the item or tool for a particular vendor.
manufacturer.title: Manufacturer

promdeliverytime.description: Promised delivery time (lead time) from the vendor for this item, tool, or service and this specific manufacturer, model, and catalog number.
promdeliverytime.title: Promised Lead Time (Days)

receipttolerance.description: The percentage value of items that can be received over the amount set in the initial agreement.
receipttolerance.title: Receipt Tolerance %

vendor.description: Actual vendor from which this item, tool, service, or lot was received. This may not be the primary vendor.
vendor.title: Vendor

isdefault.description: Specifies whether the current vendor is the default for a given item in an organization. If the check box is selected, this will be the default vendor used on Work Order Planned Materials and Services lines for direct issue requisitioning. If the check box is cleared (the default), this vendor is not the default vendor for this item. You can have only one default vendor for an item in an organization.
isdefault.title: Default Vendor

catalogwebpage.description: Internet address for this catalog item, tool, or service on the vendor's web page. Click the Detail Menu button to connect to the web page.
catalogwebpage.title: Catalog Web Page

modelnum.description: Primary vendor's model number for the item or tool.
modelnum.title: Model

lastcost.description: Last price received from the vendor for this item, tool, or service and this specific manufacturer, model, and catalog number.
lastcost.title: Last Price

orderunit.description: Order unit associated with the vendor's bid price for this item, tool, or service and this specific manufacturer, model, and catalog number. For example, the vendor sells the item at an order unit of EACH for this price. Click the Select Value button to view a list of order units.
orderunit.title: Order Unit

taxexempt.description: Select this check box if the item, the tool, or the service is not taxable. The buyer for your organization determines the tax-exempt status of items, of tools, and of services.
taxexempt.title: Tax Exempt

catalogcode.description: Vendor's catalog or part number for the item, tool, or service.
catalogcode.title: Catalog #

tax1code.description: Tax code for this vendor, for example, NY for New York. This field defaults to the tax code of the vendor in the Companies application, if there is one.
tax1code.title: Tax Code

orderunit.description: Unit of measure used to order the item, such as DOZEN or CASE. Click the Select Value button to view a list of valid order units.
orderunit.title: Order Unit

statusdate.description: Date the work order status was last changed.
statusdate.title: Status Date

capitalized.description: Specifies whether the item is to be financially accounted for as a capitalized cost or a non-capitalized cost. If the check box is selected, the item is capitalized. If the check box is cleared (the default), the item is a non-capitalized cost. Tools are always accounted for as capitalized costs. You can only change the setting for this field by using the Change Capitalized Status action in Item Master. Capitalized items are always issued and returned at zero cost, regardless of the cost carried in inventory. Non-capitalized items are issued and returned at the standard, last, or average cost, as specified in Multisite.
capitalized.title: Capitalized

opmodeor.name: opmodeor
opmodeor.description: if set to 1, this will cause the oslc.where clause to set the query mode to OR clause instead of AND.

oslc.properties.name: oslc.properties
oslc.properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource (like an individual workorder).

transactionid.name: transactionid
transactionid.description: This is used to prevent de-duplication for create/update/delete. If this header is present then the value is compared to a list of committed transaction ids.If it has a match, then it is considered as a duplicate transaction and an HTTP conflict error is generated.

oslc.where.name: oslc.where
oslc.where.description: Where clause to filter the set of resources

x-allow-events.name: x-allow-events
x-allow-events.description: For a create/update/delete, if the request wants to suppress the object events, this request header needs to be set to 0. By default all events are fired unless programmatically stopped.

x-method-override.name: x-method-override
x-method-override.description: Due to constraints from browser the HTTP POST method is re-used for creates and updates and deletes.This request header is used to convey the specifics of the POST request. PATCH is used for update. DELETE is used for deleting the resource. BULK is used for supporting bulk operations.If this header is not present, the POST request is treated as a create request.

gbrange.name: gbrange
gbrange.description: The range clause for the group by query.

lean.name: lean
lean.description: If set to 1, this will instruct the api to work with the "lean" json format as opposed to the namespaced json that it produces by default. This option is supported only starting 7601 release of the Maximo base. Its preferred to use this format as it reduces the size of the json payload.

oslc.select.name: oslc.select
oslc.select.description:
Filter clause following the OSLC query syntax
(see below for more details on this)

gbcols.name: gbcols
gbcols.description: The comma separated group by columns.

content-type.name: content-type
content-type.description: The mime-type of the request document.

gbsortby.name: gbsortby
gbsortby.description: The order by clause for the group by query. Follows the same syntax as oslc.orderBy.

id.name: id
id.description: Unique id for object structure resource

collectioncount.name: collectioncount
collectioncount.description: If set to 1, it will instruct the api to include the total count of records that matched the query ( responseInfo.totalCount). By default it will not return that unless the maximo property mxe.oslc.collectioncount is set to 1. This is a recommended way to control collection count at the request level.

oslc.searchTerms.name: oslc.searchTerms
oslc.searchTerms.description: Record wide text search for tokens specified as this query parameter value. Example oslc.searchTerms="pump","broken"

oslc.paging.name: oslc.paging
oslc.paging.description: Instruct the server to page the query response based on the default page size. The default page size needs to be configured on the server side for this to take effect.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: Set to the users apikey. This is used when the 'api' context is used (instead of 'oslc'). Do make sure that the apikey is generated for the user. You can use the Administration app to generate an apikey

oslc.pageSize.name: oslc.pageSize
oslc.pageSize.description: Instruct the server to page the query result with the specified page size.

stablepaging.name: stablepaging
stablepaging.description: If set to true, will instruct the server to page the resource from a in session MboSet.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: The mime-type of the accept document.

patchtype.name: patchtype
patchtype.description: This request header with the value of MERGE will instruct the server to treat a PATCH request (POST with x-method-override as PATCH) processor to merge the child objects (array of child objects) in the request with the corresponding child elements in the server. If this header is not present, the server will delete the child objects that are missing from the request.
